description_long: This fence object consists of three distinct sections: a rustic wooden hurdle, a low stacked-branch barrier, and a festive archway adorned with triangular pennant flags. The wood appears weathered and partially covered in moss, giving it a natural, aged look suitable for parks or outdoor event spaces. The archway is supported by two upright posts and strung with a line of colorful flags, adding a decorative and celebratory touch. The hurdle and stacked-branch sections are constructed from rough, uneven logs and branches, lashed together in a simple, utilitarian style. The overall structure is low in height and not designed for high security, but rather for demarcation or crowd control. The combination of natural wood and festive elements suggests use in community gardens, outdoor fairs, or rustic urban parks.
description: A rustic wooden fence set with a festive pennant archway, mossy hurdle, and stacked-branch barrier, featuring weathered wood and colorful flags.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three sections: the pennant archway on the left, the mossy hurdle in the center, and the stacked-branch barrier on the right.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of each section and the vertical alignment of the posts and rails.
description_view_2: Rear view with the stacked-branch barrier on the left, the hurdle in the center, and the pennant archway on the right.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness of the structures and the arrangement of the posts and rails.
